items.q: Why is this way of traveling worth it?
items.a: Choose with clarity, not by luck — guide profiles, reviews and specialties are laid out so you pick the right person in minutes. Travel into memories, not a rushed itinerary — you see who a trip suits and what you'll remember, not just the spots. With a local leading the way, you waste less time — a guide who knows the area helps you avoid traps and stick to reliable routes.

items.q: What is a private guided tour?
items.a: A private guided tour is a small-group experience led by a platform-verified local guide. The guide designs the itinerary, and travelers explore at their own pace without following a big-group schedule.

items.q: How do you ensure guide quality and safety?
items.a: Every guide is identity-verified (KYC); some also hold first-aid certification, environmental-education credentials and other professional qualifications. The platform also offers an emergency hotline with a 30-minute response.
All payments are encrypted via ECPay or LINE Pay — your credit card details never pass through this site.
Yes. Every tour has a clear refund policy. Most tours are fully refundable up to 168 hours before departure, and 70% refundable between 72 and 168 hours before. See each tour page for details.
It depends on the tour. Every tour page notes who it suits and who it may not suit — please check before booking. Some tours have a family-friendly tag.

items.q: How do I become a guide?
items.a: Click “Become a Guide” and fill out the application. Once reviewed by the platform, you can list your tours. We welcome guides with local character and professional backgrounds.

closing.title: Your Midao story starts here
closing.desc: Let a local take you deep into Taiwan and meet the real beauty of it.

tours.title: Chaishan Nature Park Caving Half-Day Tour

faq.a: No — the MVP routes focus on beginner-to-intermediate, safe exploration.

faq.q: What should I wear on the day?
faq.a: We recommend non-slip shoes, long trousers and a flexible top — avoid sandals.

cultureHistory.metaDescription: Step into Taiwan's old streets, temple plazas and indigenous communities, and let a local guide turn the stories behind a district and its people into living memories. Small-group, in-depth cultural experiences led by ID-verified guides.

cultureHistory.ogDescription: Not a quick walk-through. Follow someone who knows the stories and truly get to know a district that has lived a hundred years — and its people.
cultureHistory.heroTitle: Into districts and communities that have lived a century
cultureHistory.heroSubtitle: Not a check-in, walk-through tour — follow a local who knows the stories, and turn the people and history behind old streets, temple plazas and communities into memories you can truly feel.
cultureHistory.feature1b: People who live here help you read the place
cultureHistory.feature2b: A gentle pace, great for families and three generations together

tours.title: Indigenous Culture & Traditional Crafts Half-Day Experience

faq.q: Do I need to do homework to follow along?
faq.a: No — the guide brings you into the history through everyday life and people, so you can engage even with no background.

faq.q: Is there a lot of walking?
faq.a: It's mostly a slow walking tour, with stops and breaks along the way — suitable for most fitness levels.

faq.q: Is it suitable for elders and children together?
faq.a: Yes — the gentle pace and high interaction make it a great choice for families and three generations traveling together.

ecology.metaDescription: Follow a local nature guide into wetlands, tidal flats and forests to meet Taiwan's birds, intertidal life and woodland ecosystems. Small-group, in-depth walks led by someone who knows the land.
ecology.ogTitle: Wildlife & Nature — Read the Land Beneath Your Feet With a Local | Midao
ecology.ogDescription: Not just looking at scenery, but understanding the land you stand on. Follow a nature guide to meet Taiwan's birds, tidal life and woodland ecosystems.
ecology.heroTitle: Read a landscape with a local
ecology.heroSubtitle: Not just scenery, but living nature. Follow a guide who knows the wild into wetlands, tidal flats and forests, and meet the life on this island one quiet observation at a time.
ecology.feature1a: A local who knows the wild
ecology.feature1b: Recognizes the birds, tidal life and native plants
ecology.feature2b: Small groups that stop to observe instead of rushing
ecology.feature3b: Low-impact principles — see the land without disturbing it

tours.title: Forest Night Walk & Firefly Ecology Tour

faq.q: Can I join with no nature background?
faq.a: Yes — the guide starts from scratch. What matters is looking slowly and staying curious; no prior knowledge needed.
Wear comfortable shoes, a hat and sun/insect protection; birdwatching or night-walk gear is provided by the guide as the tour requires.
Outdoor nature tours may be adjusted or rescheduled for weather — the guide confirms with you before departure, prioritizing safety and good observation conditions.

mountainWilderness.metaDescription: Hidden 100-peak trails, forest bathing and Taiwan's grand ridgeline views — follow a local guide who knows the mountains deep into the wilds on foot. Small-group hiking led by safety-certified guides.
mountainWilderness.ogTitle: Mountain Wilds — Deep Into Taiwan's Mountains and Forests | Midao
mountainWilderness.ogDescription: No cable cars, no tour buses — just your own feet and someone who knows the mountains, walking into mountain trails no map points you to.
mountainWilderness.heroTitle: Deep into Taiwan's mountains and forests

mountainWilderness.feature1b: From gentle trails to ridgeline traverses — choose by your fitness
mountainWilderness.feature2b: Your guide introduces the vegetation and mountain stories along the way

faq.q: Can I join with no hiking experience?
faq.a: Yes — beginner routes focus on gentle trails, and the guide gives a pre-trip briefing and gear check first.

faq.q: Do I need to bring my own gear?
faq.a: We recommend bringing your own hiking shoes, water and rain gear; some tours provide trekking poles and other gear — see the tour page for details.

faq.q: Will the tour run if it rains?
faq.a: It depends on the day's weather and mountain-safety conditions — the guide will assess whether to adjust the route or reschedule.

riverTrekking.metaDescription: Step into Taiwan's purest wild streams and feel the power of Hualien's valleys. Small-group, in-depth river-trekking experiences led from start to finish by local guides.

riverTrekking.ogDescription: More than splashing around. Follow a guide who knows the streams into Hualien's most beautiful valleys.
riverTrekking.heroSubtitle: No cable cars, no tour buses — just your own feet and someone who knows the mountains, for the most authentic water-trail scenery in Taiwan.
riverTrekking.feature1b: Safety shoes, helmet and protective gear all provided
riverTrekking.feature2b: From Level 1 beginner to Level 3 advanced
riverTrekking.feature3b: Backed by outdoor-teaching and safety-training experience

tours.title: Hualien Xiuguluan River Full-Day River-Trekking Adventure

faq.q: Can I join if I can't swim?
faq.a: You can join Level 1 routes — the guide gives a safety briefing and gear check first.

faq.q: Which months are best for river trekking?
faq.a: Late spring through autumn is more stable, but it always depends on the day's water and safety conditions.

faq.q: What's the difference between Level 1 and Level 3?
faq.a: Mainly the terrain difficulty, fitness demands and the technical skill needed to get through.

about.metaDescription: Midao isn't an invented place. It's the Taiwan that's already out there — just waiting for you to see it. Get to know the story and beliefs behind Midao 祕島.
about.ogDescription: Locals who've truly walked these mountains and forests, leading you into the most storied corners of Taiwan.
about.heroSubtitle: We believe the best travel isn't rushing through sights with a tour group — it's finding someone who knows the way, who leads you into places with a real story to tell.
about.storyP1: Midao isn't an invented place. It's the Taiwan that's already out there — just waiting for you to see it.
about.storyP2a: Here you'll find locals who have truly walked these mountains and forests. No scripts, no rushing — they simply take you, once, to that one trail, that one peak, that one stream valley they know best.
about.storyP2b: We believe the value of travel isn't in how many places you've been, but in whether you've truly stepped inside.
about.storyP3: What Midao does is simple: let great local guides be seen, and let travelers book directly, pay with confidence, and enjoy a quality experience. We start with the most distinctive journeys — cave exploration on Chaishan in Kaohsiung, the old streets of Taipei, river tracing in Hualien — and grow across all of Taiwan from there.
about.ctaHeading: Let's bring Taiwan's great guides into the light, together

faq.metaDescription: Answers to common questions about booking, payment, cancellation and refunds, guide quality, and safety.
faq.ogDescription: How do you book a private guide? Is payment secure? Can you cancel? Every question, answered.
faq.introA: Can't find an answer? Feel free to

items.q: How do I book a guided journey?
items.a: Choose a journey, tap "Book now," fill in your contact details, and complete payment. We support credit cards and LINE Pay.

items.q: How soon do I get confirmation after paying?
items.a: Once payment succeeds, the system instantly sends a confirmation email. Your guide will usually reach out within 24 hours to confirm the details.

items.q: Can I hold a journey and pay later?
items.a: For now, a booking is only confirmed once payment is complete. We suggest finalizing your order after your departure time is set, so you don’t miss out if spots fill up.

Cancel 168 hours or more before departure for a full refund; cancel more than 72 but less than 168 hours before for a 70% refund. Cancellations within 72 hours of departure are, as a rule, non-refundable. For force majeure or organizer cancellations, travelers may choose a full refund or one free reschedule. Refund processing fees are absorbed by the platform — travelers pay nothing extra.

items.q: What if the guide cancels the journey?
items.a: If a cancellation is due to the guide or the platform, you receive a full refund, and we help you rearrange or recommend an alternative journey.

items.q: Can I cancel due to bad weather?
items.a: If weather conditions threaten safety, the guide may cancel and issue a full refund. Please confirm the weather with your guide before departure.
Every guide must pass real-name KYC identity verification and platform review, confirming their guiding credentials and background.

items.q: Can I request a guide language?
items.a: Each guide’s page lists their service languages. Today most guides offer service in Traditional Chinese, and some can guide in English.

items.q: How do I review a guide?
items.a: After the activity ends, you’ll receive a review invitation. We encourage travelers to share honest experiences to help others make better choices.

items.q: Can I book a private group (company or family)?
items.a: Absolutely! Reach out via the "Contact Us" page or contact a guide directly, and we’ll tailor a private-group journey for you.

items.q: What if something happens during the journey?
items.a: If there’s an emergency on the day, contact your guide directly. The platform emergency hotline also responds within 30 minutes.

items.q: How do I apply to become a guide?
items.a: Head to the "Become a Guide" page and fill out the application form. Once approved, you can list your journeys.

faq.footerSubtitle: Our support team replies within 1–2 business days

why-private-guide.title: Why hire a private guide in Taiwan instead of joining a group tour?
why-private-guide.excerpt: From efficiency to depth, the fundamental gap between two ways of traveling. Only when you're no longer chained to an itinerary can you truly get to know a place.
why-private-guide.content:
Most people picture a group tour as a coach carrying 30 people rushing between sights, snapping photos and getting back on the bus. But in a place like Taiwan — rich in cultural detail, where the alleys are more interesting than the main roads — is a group tour really the best choice?

## What a private guide changes

### You control the pace
A group itinerary is fixed: what time you arrive, how long you stop, when you leave. A private guide's plan follows you entirely — stuck in a long queue at a stall in Ningxia Night Market? No problem, the rest of the day just shifts a little.

### You hear stories, not a script
An ordinary guide has to look after 30 people, so the commentary gets shouted out and rushed through. A private guide can chat with you, answer your questions, and even take you into the little alleys and the upper floors of old houses that group tourists never reach.

### You come away with memories, not a checklist
Many of the guides on our platform grew up here. What they show you isn't a list of "tourist spots" but their own lives — how the fabric shops of Dadaocheng reinvented themselves, what creatures live in Chaishan's caves, why Hualien's streams run so clear. These are things no guidebook will tell you.

## When is a private guide right for you?

- It's your first time in Taiwan and you want to go deep, efficiently
- You're traveling with family or kids and need a flexible itinerary
- You're especially interested in a theme (food, history, outdoor adventure)
- You'd rather not be packed onto a bus with a crowd of strangers

## How our platform helps

Every guide is identity-verified, with transparent tour pricing and a clear refund policy. Before you book, you can see the guide's real reviews, what the tour includes, and feedback from other travelers.

Not sure? Just browse first, then decide once you find a tour that interests you.

chaishan-cave-guide.title: The complete guide to caving at Kaohsiung's Chaishan: nail it on your first try
chaishan-cave-guide.excerpt: How should you prep your gear? How do you pick a route? Can you bring kids? One article covering every detail of caving at Chaishan.
chaishan-cave-guide.content:
Chaishan (Shoushan) in Kaohsiung hides dozens of limestone caves — one of the few places in Taiwan where you can go caving right at the edge of the city. This article covers everything you need to know for your first time.

## Basic gear

Gear you'll need for caving:
- **Headlamp** (essential — it's pitch black inside)
- **Helmet** (limestone cave ceilings are uneven)
- **Gloves** (for gripping the rock walls)
- **Non-slip or sport shoes** (it's damp inside)
- **Long sleeves and trousers** (against insects and scrapes)

If you join Andy Lee's tour, the basic gear is all provided.

## Who is it for?

Chaishan caving suits anyone aged 6 and up with basic fitness. No climbing experience is needed, but you should be able to crouch, bend, and occasionally squeeze sideways through narrow passages. If you have severe claustrophobia, assess carefully first.

## Best season

You can cave year-round, but October to the following April is the most comfortable (cooler, fewer insects). In the rainy season (June–August) some caves may flood, and the guide will adjust the route as conditions require.

## Safety notes

- Never enter a cave alone — the risk of getting lost is very high
- Follow the guide's instructions at all times
- Don't touch the stalactites inside (they take tens of thousands of years to form and break at a touch)
- Watch your step and your head

## Recommended tour

On our platform, Andy Lee is the Chaishan caving expert — a ranger at Shoushan National Nature Park who has led hundreds of groups. The tour runs about 3–4 hours, NT$2,000 per person.

orderDetail.messagesReadonly: Messaging for this order is now read-only (14 days after the trip ends, or once the order is cancelled/refunded). Please contact support if you need help.
orderDetail.messageSendFailedRetry: Failed to send message. Please try again later.
orderDetail.reviewPhotoMaxError: You can upload at most {max} photos
orderDetail.reviewPhotoUploadFailedRetry: Photo upload failed. Please try again later.
orderDetail.reviewSubmitFailedRetry: Failed to submit review. Please try again later.
orderDetail.departurePassedNoRefund: The trip has already started/ended, so online refund requests aren't available. Please contact support.
orderDetail.refundSuccess: ✅ Refund request submitted. The amount will be returned to your original payment method (usually 3-5 business days).
orderDetail.refundFailedRetry: Refund request failed. Please try again later.
orderDetail.cancelDialogBody: Cancellation can't be undone, and your spot will be released automatically.
orderDetail.cancelFailedRetry: Cancellation failed. Please try again later.
orderDetail.rescheduleFailedRetry: Reschedule request failed. Please try again later.
orderDetail.withdrawFailedRetry: Withdrawal failed. Please try again later.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Set the nickname and region shown publicly, plus your notification preferences.
profile.emailLabel: Email (managed by your login account; cannot be changed here)
profile.displayNamePlaceholder: Default display name for reviews and order contact
profile.marketingHelper: Transactional notifications (order placed, payment, refund progress) are essential service messages and are not affected by this toggle.
profile.lineDesc: Once bound, look up your orders and payment anytime in LINE. Order notifications are sent via Email / Telegram.
profile.telegramDesc: Once bound, order placed / payment / cancellation / refund updates are also sent to your Telegram.
profile.saveFailedRetry: Save failed. Please try again later.

notificationBinding.mintFailed: Failed to generate the binding link. Please try again later.
notificationBinding.networkError: Network error. Please try again later.

orderPay.loginRequiredError: Please sign in to view this order's payment details.
orderPay.authRequiredBody: You need to sign in to view and pay for this order.
orderPay.notFound: Order not found, or this account has no access (the email may not match the one used to book).
orderPay.ecpayNotice: Clicking "Pay with ECPay" will redirect you to the ECPay secure payment page.

checkout.title: Checkout
checkout.legacyNoticeBody: You are on the legacy fallback flow. Most travelers should use the new /booking flow; this page is kept only for compatibility and failover.
checkout.legacyNoticeLink: Switch to the new booking flow (/booking)
checkout.noSchedules: ⚠️ This trip currently has no bookable schedules
checkout.emailNotice: The order confirmation email will be sent to the address above

bookingFlow.noSchedulesAvailable: There are no bookable schedules right now, please try again later.
bookingFlow.notePlaceholder: e.g. food allergies, limited mobility, would like to stay longer at a spot, etc.
bookingFlow.agreePrefix: I have read and agree to the
bookingFlow.ecpayNoticeShort: After confirming you will be redirected to the ECPay secure payment page; the actual available payment methods are shown on that page.
bookingFlow.ecpayEncryptNotice: 🔒 Payment is encrypted and processed by ECPay; data does not pass through this site
bookingFlow.errFillContactAndAgree: Please complete the contact info, select a bookable slot, and agree to the terms first.
bookingFlow.errNoOrderYet: No order created yet, please go back and create the order first.
bookingFlow.v2GenericError: Unable to load available dates right now, please try again later.
bookingFlow.v2PlanRecovery: This plan could not be found, please go back to the trip page and select again.
bookingFlow.missingPlanParam: Missing or unresolvable plan parameter, please go back to the trip page and select a plan.
bookingFlow.lineFallbackMissingPlan: The LINE LIFF continuation flow was interrupted (missing or unresolvable plan). Please return to the LIFF entry, re-enter the full parameters, and try again.
bookingFlow.missingPlanRetry: Please go back to the trip page, select a plan, and try again.
bookingFlow.v2RecoveryLink: Go back to the trip page to select a plan again
bookingFlow.lineRetryFallback: The LINE LIFF continuation flow keeps the shared checkout/payment-init; it does not switch to the legacy flow. Please retry; if it keeps failing, report the Correlation ID:
bookingFlow.minParticipantsNote: This trip requires a minimum of {min} guests
bookingFlow.overCapacity: The number of participants exceeds the remaining capacity for this date, please reduce the number or choose another date.
bookingFlow.requestBookingHint: This trip uses "request to book": after you submit, the guide reviews it, and once approved you'll be notified to pay. You will not be charged at this step.
bookingFlow.requestSubmittedBody: Once the guide approves, we'll notify you by email to pay; you have not been charged at this step. You can check the status under "My orders".
bookingFlow.transferSubmittedBody: Please complete the transfer per the bank details; we'll manually verify the payment and confirm your booking. You can check the status under "My orders".
bookingFlow.ecpayTransferNotice: After confirming you will be redirected to the ECPay secure payment page; the actual available payment methods are shown on that page. 🔒 Payment is encrypted and processed by ECPay; data does not pass through this site.
bookingFlow.transferNotConfigured: This guide has not provided bank transfer details yet, please use a credit card instead.
bookingFlow.transferCompleteNote: After completing the transfer, press the button below to submit; we'll manually verify the payment.
bookingFlow.errBackToCreateOrder: Please go back to create the order first, then confirm payment.
bookingFlow.errSlotUnavailable: This slot cannot be booked right now, please refresh or choose another date.
bookingFlow.errOrderNotComplete: The order is not fully created yet, please go back and recreate the order.
